member.office_address: 506 Cannon House Office Building Washington DC 20515-0534

form_analysis.form_header_text: Office of Rep. Gomez (CA-34) - FY 2027 Programmatic/Language Appropriations Request Form
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: Thank you for your interest in submitting an appropriations request with the Office of Congressman Jimmy Gomez. Please note that this form is for programmatic and language requests only. If Community Project Funding is announced for FY 27, separate submission forms and processes will be provided in accordance with committee guidance on our Appropriations page (https://gomez.house.gov/constituent-services/appropriations.htm) under the Community Project Funding header. If you have multiple requests, please submit them separately. If you have any questions, please contact us at CA34.Appropriations@mail.house.gov.

fields.label: Request Type
fields.rationale: The request is for a specific funding level for the Science Mission Directorate, which is a programmatic funding request.

fields.label: Short�Title
fields.rationale: Standard short title for this funding account as provided in the reference values.

fields.label: Select the Appropriations Bill
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration
Labor, Health and Human Services, Education
fields.rationale: NASA is funded through the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies subcommittee. This option is the closest match in the provided list.

fields.label: Account
fields.rationale: The specific account within NASA is Science (Science Mission Directorate).

fields.label: Program Title
fields.rationale: This is the top-line program/directorate name.

fields.label: Is this a new or existing program?
fields.rationale: The Science Mission Directorate is an established, existing part of NASA.

fields.label: Program's funding level in FY 26:
fields.help_text: Enter the numeric dollar amount enacted for the prior fiscal year.
fields.rationale: This is the FY2026 enacted level provided in the reference values.

fields.label: President's�FY 27 Budget Request:
fields.rationale: The FY2027 President's Budget Request has not been released yet.

fields.label: Amount you're requesting for FY 27:
fields.rationale: This is the target funding ask for the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Requested funding action
options: Support the President's FY 27 budget request
fields.rationale: The request specifies a total top-line funding amount for the account.

fields.label: Language type
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic funding request, not a request for specific bill or report language.

fields.label: Draft�language:
fields.rationale: This value matches the exact programmatic funding ask for the Science Mission Directorate as specified in the pre-computed reference values.

fields.label: Describe problem or issue addressed through this request and how it will benefit California's 34th District? (Max 300 words)
fields.draft_value:
* Funding addresses the erosion of U.S. space leadership and restores stability to the scientific workforce after damaging cuts in FY 2026.
* Supports CA-34 through $6.3M+ in recent NASA Science obligations, including $2.9M at USC for autonomous planetary robotics and $537k at Cal State LA for Moon/Mars exploration.
* Strengthens the local aerospace talent pipeline by funding remote sensing research and strategic science programs at Cal State LA University Auxiliary Services.
* Every dollar invested in NASA Science returns roughly $3 to the economy, driving innovation across the 15 active grants currently held by district institutions.
fields.rationale: This response links the $9B national ask to specific local impacts in CA-34, citing the $6.3M total spending and specific projects at USC and Cal State LA found in the spending context.

fields.label: List all other organizations supporting your request, if any:
fields.draft_value:
* American Astronomical Society (AAS)
* American Geophysical Union (AGU)
fields.rationale: These organizations are listed as allied organizations supporting the NASA Science funding increase in the advocacy reference values.

fields.label: List other Members of Congress you are submitting this request to, if any:
fields.draft_value:
* Representative Don Bacon (R-NE)
* Representative Judy Chu (D-CA)
fields.rationale: These members are identified as the leads for the House multi-member letter supporting NASA Science in the reference context.

fields.label: If you are submitting more than one FY 26 appropriations request, please list them below and rank by priority (with 1 being the highest).
fields.rationale: Priority ranking depends on the submitter's full portfolio of requests.

fields.label: Are you requesting Rep. Gomez to cosign an appropriations letter in support of this request?
fields.rationale: An existing House letter led by Reps. Bacon and Chu is available for cosigning.

fields.label: What office is leading the letter?
fields.draft_value: Reps. Don Bacon and Judy Chu
fields.rationale: The reference values identify these members as the leads for the House letter.

fields.label: Is your organization located in or does it operate primarily in the 34th Congressional District of California?
Is your organization located in or does it operate in California?

top_grants.recipient_name: Cal State La University Auxiliary Services Inc
top_grants.description: Execute a balanced science program based on discipline-specific guidance from the national academies of sciences  engineering  and medicine  administration priorities  and direction from congress. Participate as a key partner and enabler in the agency S exploration initiative  focusing on scientific research of  on  and from the moon  lunar orbit  Mars  and beyond. Advance discovery in emerging fields by identifying and exploiting cross-disciplinary opportunities between traditional science disciplines. Develop a directorate-wide  target-user focused approach to applied programs  including Earth science applications  space weather  planetary defense  and space situational awareness.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Supporting strategic science for Moon, Mars exploration and planetary defense.
